escalation.rationale: Marginal de-escalation as Lebanon-Israel talks progress and Trump reversed Hormuz shipping levy, but Iran signals Bab el-Mandeb closure via Houthis, Houthis strike Saudi Arabia, US resumes Iran strikes after MoU collapse, and Russia continues ballistic missile attacks on Ukraine, sustaining near-critical tension across two active war theaters.

ticker_items: Trump declares Islamabad MoU 'over'; US resumes strikes on Iran.
Houthis fire ballistic missiles and drones at Saudi Arabia's Abha airport.
Iran signals possible Bab el-Mandeb closure, threatening Red Sea shipping.
Russia launches ballistic missiles at Kyiv and Odessa; Ukraine intercepts five.
Lebanon-Israel sixth round of US-brokered talks concludes with limited progress.

new_entrant.text: Saudi Arabia has entered active hostilities as the Houthis struck Abha airport with ballistic missiles and drones, with Trump reportedly approving retaliatory Saudi strikes on Yemen.

eastern_europe.description: Russia targeted Ukrainian drone and missile production and storage sites in Kyiv with ballistic missiles overnight.
Russia struck drone and missile sites in Odessa as part of overnight multi-city attack package.

middle_east.description: Houthis fired ballistic missiles and drones at Abha airport in Saudi Arabia in retaliation for strikes on Sanaa airport.
US resumed strikes on Iran following Trump's declaration that the Islamabad MoU is over, killing dozens according to reports.
Saudi Arabia struck Sanaa airport in Yemen, reportedly with US approval, prompting Houthi retaliatory missile launches.

today.incident: Russian ballistic missile strikes on Ukraine
Houthi strikes on Saudi Arabia (Abha airport)

claimed_totals.claim: Killed in Iran (US-Israel strikes since Feb 28, 2026)
Killed in Lebanon (US-Israel regional strikes since Feb 28, 2026)
Killed in Gulf states (US-Israel regional strikes since Feb 28, 2026)

russia.text: Kiev's European backers cannot play a role in settling the Ukraine conflict while excluding Russia.
russia.speaker: Kremlin Spokesperson
russia.title: Kremlin

ukraine.text: The speed of military innovation has itself become a decisive factor of military power.
ukraine.speaker: Vice Chief of Germany's Army
ukraine.title: German Army

middle_east.text: The Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding is over.
middle_east.speaker: Donald Trump
middle_east.title: US President

middle_east.text: Bulgaria has no place in the coalition supporting more military aid to Ukraine; diplomacy is needed instead of further escalation.
middle_east.speaker: Rumen Radev
middle_east.title: Prime Minister of Bulgaria

middle_east.text: Iran is now signaling it could play its most dangerous card yet: using Yemen's Houthi allies to threaten the Bab el-Mandeb.
middle_east.speaker: Mohammed al-Bukhaiti
middle_east.title: Senior Houthi Official

observers.description: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev announced Bulgaria is withdrawing from the coalition of Ukraine military aid supporters. Radev called for diplomacy over further military escalation, signaling a significant shift in Sofia's posture within NATO.

Saudi Arabia struck Sanaa airport in Yemen, reportedly with US approval, in a major escalation against Houthi forces. Houthis retaliated with ballistic missiles and drones targeting Abha airport, and have threatened a full 'siege' on Saudi Arabia.

Pakistan faces its most difficult Iran tightrope yet as the US-Iran war intensifies and Houthi-Saudi hostilities expand. A recent Saudi-Pakistan defense pact complicates Islamabad's longstanding neutrality between Riyadh and Tehran.

North Korea's activities near the DMZ are raising concern in South Korea, with Seoul warning of possible armistice violations. Pyongyang is emboldened by its security and economic alliance with Moscow and flush with revenue from troop deployments to Russia.

China and North Korea marked the 65th anniversary of their friendship treaty this week. China's posture remains one of strategic observation, as its North Korean ally grows increasingly assertive along the Korean peninsula's DMZ.

The UAE is reportedly acquiring ex-Turkish S-400 systems, which would give it the most diversified air defense architecture in the region. This move reflects Gulf states' hedging strategies as the US-Iran war reshapes regional security.

Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham is being briefed by senior civil servants on a worsening economy and rising interest rates, with a potential oil price shock flagged if the Iran war continues. The UK government also added democratic interference and cyberattacks to the National Risk Register.

financial.notes: Bitcoin ticked up to ~$64K following the largest US inflation slowdown in six years, but geopolitical tensions from the US-Iran war and Strait of Hormuz/Bab el-Mandeb disruptions continue to suppress risk appetite; incoming UK PM faces warnings of an oil price shock if the Iran war drags on.

update_log.text: Iran installs billboard in Tehran depicting US President Trump lying in a coffin, amid resumed US airstrikes.
Al Jazeera maps US strikes across Iran; attacks described as killing dozens and pushing fragile ceasefire closer to collapse.
Lebanon and Israel conclude sixth round of US-brokered talks; some progress reported on Israeli withdrawal from pilot zones in southern Lebanon.
Iran signals potential closure of the Bab el-Mandeb Strait via Houthi allies, threatening Red Sea shipping routes after Hormuz disruption.
BBC confirms Houthis launched ballistic missiles and drones at Saudi Arabia's Abha airport after Saudi strikes on Sanaa.
Al Jazeera reports leading Houthi official Mohammed al-Bukhaiti threatens a full 'siege' on Saudi Arabia.
Trump declared the Islamabad MoU 'over' at NATO's Ankara summit, with US forces resuming attacks on Iran.
Trump reversed a planned levy on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz as US and Iran exchange strikes in the waterway.
Bulgaria's PM Radev announces withdrawal from Ukraine military aid coalition, calling for diplomacy over escalation.
War on the Rocks analyst assessment: NATO's Ankara summit exposed unity on paper but significant fractures beneath the surface on Ukraine, Iran, and burden-sharing.
Ukraine's air force reports intercepting five Russian ballistic missiles in overnight attacks targeting Kyiv and Odessa.
War on the Rocks publishes analysis warning Russia's mass force expansion is reaching the limits of quantitative advantage in Ukraine.
South Korea formally expresses concern over North Korean DMZ activities as potential breach of the 1953 armistice agreement.
